[2020-12-15 16:29] zera777 (Zera): Idea: The Prismata multiplayer takes place long after the events of the campaign. Therefore, the fictional consistency does not require you to keep the units consistent between modes. Boom, workload saved.
[2020-12-15 16:29] zera777 (Zera): BTW, when is Chapter 4 out?
~~Do I need to upgrade my support tier?~~
[2020-12-15 16:31] velizar_ (Name): The problem is that the campaign can serve as a gentle introduction to the units, and overall it's just messier to have two different variants of units on campaign versus multiplayer - the problem isn't in lore, it's in players learning to use the wrong units.
[2020-12-15 16:32] velizar_ (Name): Starcraft 2 does this, it has slightly different variants of the same units in campaign vs co-op vs multiplayer, and I find it quite annoying.
[2020-12-15 16:34] zera777 (Zera): To that, I say
1. The campaign already has exclusive player and enemy units. (Do the puzzle packs as well?)
2. There are already 100+ Random Set units. Prismata jumped the fence on learnability long ago.
[2020-12-15 16:34] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Although I agree it can be annoying, for me having a non-base-set unit show up in one or two scenarios/puzzles never taught me much about the unit and more about general tactics and strategy.
[2020-12-15 16:35] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Also, most of the times you play against custom units, so it's not like you'll learn how to play a unit in the ladder meta.
[2020-12-15 16:38] velizar_ (Name): 1. - fair, those don't hurt, Starcraft 2 has them and they don't hurt either; maybe it's a problem when a unit is very similar, or maybe it's just a loss of opportunity - it is very good to be able to learn more units from campaign, rather than fewer
2. learnability is not infinite, it just takes a while, if it's easier then that's better
[2020-12-15 16:38] velizar_ (Name): I think there might be some circumstances where it's worth changing a unit in multiplayer without changing it in campaign, while in others it's better to not change it at all, or to only change it in both - all of those can be valid depending on circumstances
[2020-12-15 16:43] velizar_ (Name): One of the charm of Starcraft 2 is that I get to play with the same units under completely different circumstances, it's almost like a different game - similar to Event games on Prismata - I love the variety; having different units diminishes some of that variety, because you no longer reuse your skills in a completely different context, instead you do something slightly different. It's still pretty good, just not as good as it can be.
[2020-12-15 16:43] velizar_ (Name): This argument applies less to Prismata, but I think it might still apply. That's because I feel that Starcraft 2 has more mind-blowing variety.

messages.author: zera777
messages.content: Idea: The Prismata multiplayer takes place long after the events of the campaign. Therefore, the fictional consistency does not require you to keep the units consistent between modes. Boom, workload saved.

messages.author: zera777
messages.content:
BTW, when is Chapter 4 out?
~~Do I need to upgrade my support tier?~~

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: The problem is that the campaign can serve as a gentle introduction to the units, and overall it's just messier to have two different variants of units on campaign versus multiplayer - the problem isn't in lore, it's in players learning to use the wrong units.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: Starcraft 2 does this, it has slightly different variants of the same units in campaign vs co-op vs multiplayer, and I find it quite annoying.

messages.author: zera777
messages.content:
To that, I say
1. The campaign already has exclusive player and enemy units. (Do the puzzle packs as well?)
2. There are already 100+ Random Set units. Prismata jumped the fence on learnability long ago.

messages.author: jeacaveo
messages.content: Although I agree it can be annoying, for me having a non-base-set unit show up in one or two scenarios/puzzles never taught me much about the unit and more about general tactics and strategy.

messages.author: jeacaveo
messages.content: Also, most of the times you play against custom units, so it's not like you'll learn how to play a unit in the ladder meta.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content:
1. - fair, those don't hurt, Starcraft 2 has them and they don't hurt either; maybe it's a problem when a unit is very similar, or maybe it's just a loss of opportunity - it is very good to be able to learn more units from campaign, rather than fewer
2. learnability is not infinite, it just takes a while, if it's easier then that's better

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: I think there might be some circumstances where it's worth changing a unit in multiplayer without changing it in campaign, while in others it's better to not change it at all, or to only change it in both - all of those can be valid depending on circumstances

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: One of the charm of Starcraft 2 is that I get to play with the same units under completely different circumstances, it's almost like a different game - similar to Event games on Prismata - I love the variety; having different units diminishes some of that variety, because you no longer reuse your skills in a completely different context, instead you do something slightly different. It's still pretty good, just not as good as it can be.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: This argument applies less to Prismata, but I think it might still apply. That's because I feel that Starcraft 2 has more mind-blowing variety.

[2020-12-18 18:02] awaclus: I have formed a very new opinion, I think we should try a lifespan Cryo
[2020-12-18 18:03] elyot: I will revert automaid
[2020-12-18 18:03] awaclus: for example at 5 lifespan
[2020-12-18 18:04] awaclus: with no hp loss was the idea, but either way is fine
[2020-12-18 18:04] elyot: 5 lifespan feels like a buff to me lol
[2020-12-18 18:05] awaclus: MasN suggested 5 and it doesn't sound ridiculous to me
[2020-12-18 18:07] awaclus: you can deny granularity/absorb more often but if the opponent defends it, they end up with 1 extra defense after it dies
[2020-12-18 18:08] awaclus: as opposed to the current Cryo which effectively trades with 1 defense plus gets two chances to deny granularity or absorb
[2020-12-18 18:10] p0lari (Polari): 5 sounds reasonable to me
[2020-12-18 18:10] p0lari (Polari): like 3 is obviously a nerf
[2020-12-18 18:10] p0lari (Polari): no way 4 isn't a nerf either
[2020-12-18 18:12] velizar_ (Name): at least the it would stop the bot from wasting it to freeze things which I won't defend on.
[2020-12-18 18:14] p0lari (Polari): ok 4 is probably a buff with valk but with no degenerate combos involved, I don't think you're realistically going to have value clicks every turn, and a big part of cryo's power is that in sets where you can't gambit, the last cryo hp is almost as good as a gauss charge
